name: Raven's Home (the Old Warden Post)
descriptionSeed: An old Ashwarden watch-post on a shoulder of ground two miles northwest along the March road's left branch — three rooms and a stair, a corner watch-tower, and a fold of thin ground behind it. Reactivated and claimed by Silas Weir and company; named for its rooms: Pell's forge, Veth's laboratory, Cassiel's keeper's ground, Huginn's Rook, and the Maker's hollow that gives Stillwater's Trouble its name.
encounterFlavor: Scavengers who assumed it was empty. Someone from the settlements below come up to see who has moved in and why. Old warden fittings worth more as iron than as history. Nothing here hunts you — but nothing here is watching the road for you either, until someone starts.
questSeeds: The Post has no owner anyone can name, which is either an opportunity or a question somebody below has already asked
The corner tower's sightlines cover the road to the treeline — whoever holds it sees who comes to Stillwater first
The hollow behind the Post is thin ground of the unreached kind, and thin ground is worth something to the wrong people too
axisVectorNote: Ashwarden death-life ground (index-8 negative, death-leaning like its region) but MODERATED — reactivated and warded as a held refuge, pulled back toward life, with order (the binding+Boundary-Stone wards), peace (a protected refuge), and space (the watch-tower sightlines, the hollow's thin unreached ground).
worldPosNote: Two miles NW of Cairnhold (colat 76, lon 74) along the Ashwarden March road's left branch; the road waypost (marker-stone fork ~1/4 mile N of the waygate) is a SEPARATE place.
description:
The old Warden Post sits on a shoulder of ground that has been holding weather off Stillwater for longer than anyone has kept records for. Three rooms and a stair, all of them wrong for what they were built for and most of them right for something else. The long room faces east and takes morning light through arrow-slits that were never meant to let anything in; the hearth-wall is thick enough to hold heat for a day.

The rooms have been named by the people who claimed them. **Pell's forge** is built into the long room's chimney-wall — a flue that has drawn for two hundred years, the stone already sound. **Veth's laboratory** is up the stair, a room with a shuttered window and a floor that slopes very slightly toward the outer wall: a fault in a barracks and a virtue anywhere something might spill, and cold in the mornings, which is useful. **Cassiel's keeper's ground** is not in the Post at all — it is the bare south terrace where the wall gives out and the light stays longest, chosen before it was needed, because choosing it after is just a burial. **Huginn's Rook** is the corner tower, or what is left of it: open to the sky on one side, three walls and a floor and a view of every approach from the road to the treeline — the only part of the Post built to watch rather than to withstand.

And below, down the back slope where the hill folds in on itself, is **the Maker's hollow** — the thin ground that gives Stillwater's Trouble its name. Not drained thin, not the deliberate quiet of ground that gave itself up; the other kind, the kind that never had anything to give, ordinary country the old works simply never reached. Silas placed a ward on the road waypost to signal the post reactivated, then arrived here and claimed it — the post, its buildings, and the hollow — and the company has begun to weave the boundary that will hold it.
